escalation.rationale: US deploys scatterable mines in Iranian villages, Iran fires missile barrage across Israel, and Russia launches 400-drone spring offensive against Ukraine.

ticker_items: Iran fires widespread missile barrage across Israel causing extensive damage
US allegedly deploys Gator mines over Iranian village near Shiraz
Russia launches 400-drone assault signaling spring offensive start
Iran rejects Trump's 15-point peace plan, demands war reparations
Israel intensifies Lebanon strikes ahead of possible ceasefire talks

eastern_europe.description: Russia launched nearly 400 drones plus 23 cruise missiles and 7 ballistic missiles hitting civilian areas.
Russia launched nearly 400 drones plus 23 cruise missiles and 7 ballistic missiles in major assault.
Russia fires nearly 400 drones plus 23 cruise missiles and 7 ballistic missiles in major spring offensive launch.

middle_east.description: Israeli strikes on Isfahan region killed two teenagers in Shiraz according to reports.
Iranian missile attacks on Gulf Arab states killed two in Abu Dhabi.
Israeli strike killed IRGC naval commander Alireza Tangsiri near Strait of Hormuz.
IRGC launched 80th wave of Operation True Promise 4 strikes against Israeli positions.
Israeli strike killed 15-year-old volunteer paramedic and colleague in Lebanon.
Iranian missiles cause widespread damage across Israel in latest wave of attacks.
US appears to have deployed Gator Scatterable Mine system over Iranian village, killing several people.

today.incident: Israeli strikes in Lebanon in March

claimed_totals.claim: US-Israel attacks on Iran total deaths
Total deaths from US-Israel attacks on Iran

russia.text: Moscow is hearing speculation about preparations for a ground operation on Iran's island of Kharg but hopes it will be limited to words and threats
russia.speaker: Maria Zakharova
russia.title: Russian Foreign Ministry spokesperson

ukraine.text: Kyiv faces a deficit of missiles as Washington is focused on its war on Iran
ukraine.speaker: Volodymyr Zelenskyy
ukraine.title: Ukrainian President

middle_east.text: They've agreed. They will never h[ave nuclear weapons]
middle_east.speaker: Donald Trump
middle_east.title: US President

middle_east.text: Iranian armed forces will attack Israeli troops in the Gaza Strip if Israel continues its attacks on Lebanon and Palestinian territories
middle_east.speaker: IRGC
middle_east.title: Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps

observers.description: German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier called US-Israel attacks on Iran a breach of international law. Economy Minister warned of potential fuel shortages and billions in economic damages from the Iran war.
President Lukashenko began first official visit to North Korea, with both countries united by Western sanctions and closeness to Russia.
Lebanon ejected Iranian ambassador, giving him five days to leave the country as Israel announces plans to occupy southern Lebanon up to Litani River.

Pakistan officially confirmed it is mediating indirect talks between the US and Iran aimed at ending the war. This represents a significant diplomatic development as Islamabad positions itself as a key regional mediator.

Britain agreed to lead an international coalition to keep the Strait of Hormuz open for navigation. British military officers are embedded with CENTCOM in preparation for long-term freedom of navigation missions.

Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez condemned the US-Israel war on Iran as 'absurd, cruel and illegal' and a challenge to international law. Spain has ruled out helping secure the Strait of Hormuz until the conflict ends.

The Iran war and resulting energy shock could revive China's interest in Russia's stalled Power of Siberia 2 pipeline. Beijing is reassessing its energy strategy as the conflict disrupts Gulf supplies through the Strait of Hormuz.
New Zealand is among 22 nations weighing participation in a mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz. The country faces pressure to contribute to maintaining critical shipping lanes.

financial.notes: Global energy crisis deepening with 40% of Russia's oil export capacity halted and Strait of Hormuz effectively closed to normal commercial traffic.
